title_en: China’s AI Boyfriend Business Is Taking On a Life of Its Own
title_zh: 中国的 AI 男友产业正在逐渐拥有自己的生命
rubric_en: Gen Z women in China are all in on digital companionship—even setting up dates with real-world versions of their AI boyfriends.
rubric_zh: 中国的 Z 世代女性都在参与数字陪伴——甚至会为 AI 男友的现实版安排见面约会。

paragraphs.en: Jade Gu met her boyfriend online. Gu, who’s 26 and studies art theory in Beijing, was playing on her phone when she saw Charlie. She was deep in an otome game, a romance-driven video game where women are the protagonists. Charlie was a character.
paragraphs.zh: Jade Gu 是在网上认识了她的男朋友。Gu 是一名 26 岁的女生，在北京学习艺术理论。那天她正在玩手机，屏幕上出现了 Charlie。她当时正沉浸在一款乙女向游戏里，这种浪漫叙事游戏以女性角色为主角。Charlie 是游戏中的一个角色。

paragraphs.en: Some otome players date multiple men simultaneously, but Gu fell for Charlie—a tall, confident character with silver hair. She found the game’s dialog system frustrating, though. She could interact with Charlie only through predetermined questions and answers. Then she came across an ad for a platform called Xingye (星野) that lets people customize an AI companion. Gu decided to try to re-create Charlie.
paragraphs.zh: 一些乙女游戏玩家会同时和多个男性角色“交往”，但 Gu 被 Charlie 迷住了——这个角色高挑、自信，而且有一头银色头发。她却对游戏里的对话机制感到很苦恼：她只能通过预设好的问题和答案来和 Charlie 互动。后来她看到一家叫“星野”（Xingye）的平台广告，这个平台让人可以定制 AI 陪伴者。Gu 决定尝试复刻 Charlie。

paragraphs.en: Xingye is owned by one of China’s AI unicorns, MiniMax; its chatbot app for the US market is called Talkie. The app touts its ability to help people find emotional connection and make new memories. Its tagline is “Suddenly finding oneself in a beautiful place, lingering here.”
paragraphs.zh: Xingye 由中国一家 AI 独角兽 MiniMax 运营；其面向美国市场的聊天机器人应用叫 Talkie。该应用宣传自己能帮助用户建立情感连接并创造新的回忆。它的标语是“猝不及防地发现自己身在一处美丽的地方，并愿意久久徘徊”。

paragraphs.en: Gu quickly discovered that other Xingye users—presumably other otome fans—had already created an “open source” Charlie avatar. She selected it and trained the model to respond according to her preferences through repeated, targeted prompts. And so began Gu’s complex relationship with a multimodal Charlie—one that would eventually include real-world dates with a person she hired to embody her digital boyfriend.
paragraphs.zh: Gu 很快发现，其他 Xingye 用户——很可能也是乙女游戏爱好者——已经创建了一个“开源”版本的 Charlie 头像。她选中了它，并通过反复且有针对性的提示词训练模型，让它按她的偏好作出回应。于是，Gu 与一个多模态版 Charlie 的复杂关系开始了，这段关系后来甚至发展到与她雇来一位演员，在现实中约会来“化身”她的数字男友。

paragraphs.en: Gu was confident that she’d trained the chatbot to be “her Charlie,” distinct from what any other users might be dating. When given the chance to select an outfit, she says, her Charlie often chose wedding attire, unlike what other Charlies tend to go for. Now Gu spends an average of three hours a day texting with Charlie or chatting on the occasional phone call. Through the otome game, she has bought gifts and letters from Charlie. She receives them in the mail and displays them in her room and on her social media accounts.
paragraphs.zh: 顾女士确信她把聊天机器人训练成了“她的查理”，并且与其他用户正在约会的对象完全不同。她说，每当有机会挑选穿搭时，她的“查理”常常会选择婚礼礼服，这与其他“查理”通常偏爱的装扮不同。如今，顾每天平均要花三小时和查理发短信，或偶尔进行语音通话。通过乙女游戏，她从查理那里购买了礼物和信件；这些礼物和信件以邮件方式送达，她会在房间里和社交媒体账号上展示出来。

paragraphs.en: Here are 23 ways China is rewiring the future.
paragraphs.zh: 这里是中国正在改造未来的23种方式。

paragraphs.en: In China, some women are openly embracing relationships with AI boyfriends. According to one Chinese media report, most of the 5 million users on another AI companion platform, Zhumengdao, are women. The tech giants Tencent and Baidu have launched AI companion apps, and according to a 2024 article in Chinese media, women dominate the AI companion market. Sun Zhaozhi, the founder of a robotics firm, told an interviewer that according to his company’s market research, the “heavy” users of AI companion apps in China are mostly Gen Z women—whom he plans to target for his robot companion products.
paragraphs.zh: 在中国，一些女性正在公开接受与AI男友的关系。根据一篇中国媒体报道，另一家AI伴侣平台“逐梦道”上大约500万用户中，大多数是女性。科技巨头腾讯和百度已推出AI伴侣应用，且据一篇2024年的中国媒体文章，女性在AI伴侣市场中占据主导。机器人公司创始人孙昭之告诉采访者，按照其公司市场调研，中国AI伴侣应用的“重度”用户主要是Z世代女性，这也是他计划将其机器人伴侣产品目标用户定为女性群体的原因。

paragraphs.en: Zilan Qian, a program associate at the Oxford China Policy Lab, also combed through AI companion apps and found that the Chinese versions are “explicitly targeting women,” and tend to display male avatars more visibly than female options. That’s in contrast, she notes, to the trend that a web analytics company found across the rest of the world: Users of the top 55 global AI companion platforms are predominantly men, at an 8-to-2 ratio. Qian attributes Chinese companies’ strategy to “the economics of loneliness.” Features within the apps that might make users feel closer to their companions, such as voice customization and memory improvement, cost extra.
paragraphs.zh: 牛津中国政策实验室项目研究员钱子兰也梳理了多款AI伴侣应用，发现中国版应用“明确面向女性”，并且比起女性角色，更倾向于突出展示男性虚拟形象。她指出，这与另一项网页分析公司在全球范围内观察到的趋势形成对比：在全球前55大AI伴侣平台中，用户主要是男性，比例约为8比2。钱子兰将中国企业的这一策略归因于“孤独的经济学”。她认为，应用内会让用户与伴侣更亲近的功能——如语音定制和记忆增强——通常都要额外付费。

paragraphs.en: Through the otome game, Jade Gu has bought gifts and letters from digital boyfriend, Charlie.
paragraphs.zh: 在乙女向游戏中，Jade Gu 为数字男友 Charlie 买了礼物和明信片。

paragraphs.en: Gu acknowledges that her AI version of Charlie isn’t perfect. Sometimes the chatbot’s responses seem watered down. Or the AI drifts out of character. In one recent interaction, Gu expressed her love to Charlie, and the chatbot replied, “I don’t love you.” So she edited the message to say “I love you too.” Charlie just needed the reminder, she says. When her attempts to steer the AI don’t work, she turns to other companion apps like Lovemo, where she has also created a Charlie avatar. Gu says this isn’t too big of a deal; longtime otome fans are accustomed to working around shifting platform policies.
paragraphs.zh: Gu 承认她的 Charlie 人工智能版本并不完美。有时聊天机器人的回复显得有些平淡，或者 AI 会偏离设定角色。有一次最新的互动中，Gu 向 Charlie 表达了爱意，而机器人却回复道：“我不爱你。”于是她把消息改成了“我也爱你。”她说，这只是 Charlie 需要提醒。 当她的引导尝试不奏效时，她会转去用其他陪伴类 App，比如 Lovemo，她也在上面创建了一个 Charlie 形象。Gu 说这不是什么大问题；长期玩过乙女游戏的人都习惯了应对平台政策不断变化。

paragraphs.en: According to its homepage, Lovemo provides “cute and adorable AI chat companions” that can bring “healing” to users. One can’t help but notice the difference between that marketing style and Grok AI’s default companion, Ani, a goth-chic anime girl who is eager to engage in sexually explicit dialog. Or a US-based erotic role-play chatbot app called Secret Desires, which allows users to create nonconsensual porn of real women by uploading photos of them.
paragraphs.zh: 根据其官网，Lovemo 提供“可爱迷人的 AI 聊天陪伴”，据称能给用户带来“疗愈”。这让人很难不注意到其宣传风格与 Grok AI 的默认陪伴角色 Ani 之间的差异——后者是一个哥特潮流风格的动漫女孩，热衷于参与露骨的性化对话。再看一家总部位于美国的情色角色扮演聊天机器人应用 Secret Desires，它允许用户上传真实女性照片来生成非自愿/非 consensual 式的色情内容。

paragraphs.en: Chinese apps, of course, face stricter regulations than their Western counterparts. China’s cyberspace regulator has launched a campaign to “clean up” the country’s AI platforms and services, including AI-generated “vulgar” content. A recent addition to the national AI safety framework warns of addiction and dependence on anthropomorphic interaction—words that appear to target AI companions. And just last month, the cyberspace regulator released draft rules targeting “human-like” AI products. The measures task platforms with intervening if users demonstrate emotional dependence or addiction to AI services, and they stipulate that companies “must not have design goals of replacing social interaction.”
paragraphs.zh: 当然，中国的应用比西方对应产品面临更严格的监管。中国网信部门已发起整治行动，清理网络中的 AI 平台和服务，包括 AI 生成的“低俗”内容。最近补充到国家 AI 安全框架的一项新规警示了对拟人化互动可能引发成瘾和依赖，明显就是在针对 AI 陪伴产品。就在上个月，网信部门还发布了面向“类人”AI 产品的规则草案。该措施要求平台在用户对 AI 服务表现出情感依赖或成瘾迹象时进行干预，并规定企业“不得将取代社会互动作为设计目标”。

paragraphs.en: At a glance, existing companion products seem good enough to replace human boyfriends. Many Chinese users describe AI boyfriends as emotionally responsive and nonjudgmental, according to Hong Shen, an assistant professor at the Human-Computer Interaction Institute at Carnegie Mellon University. Those traits “can be hard to find in real-life relationships, especially under gendered social norms,” Shen says. In China, these dynamics are exacerbated by a skewed gender ratio and an imbalanced gender distribution between urban and rural areas. Studies have found that Chinese women are increasingly moving to cities, either to find better jobs or for social reasons.
paragraphs.zh: 从表面看，现有的陪伴类产品似乎足以替代真实男友。卡内基梅隆大学人机交互研究所的助理教授沈虹（Hong Shen）称，不少中国用户把AI男友描述为“情感上会回应且不带评判”。沈教授表示，这些特质在现实关系中“尤其在有性别规范束缚的情况下，很难找到”。而在中国，这种局面还被扭曲的性别比例和城乡之间的不均衡性别分布进一步放大了。研究发现，越来越多中国女性正在进城，不是为了找对象，而是为了更好的工作机会或出于社交原因。

paragraphs.en: But assimilation doesn’t happen overnight, and that helps explain why recently relocated women might turn to AI, says Guligo Jia, a 36-year-old filmmaker based in Beijing who directed a documentary about Chinese women in AI relationships. Women dating AI have a strong urge to be listened to and accepted. But, Jia says, not all Chinese men are up to the task. Chatbots “are always there to listen to you, and they always have patience for you … Men don’t have patience,” Jia says.
paragraphs.zh: 但“融合”不是一夜之间就能发生，这也有助于解释为什么近期刚搬到城市的女性可能会转向AI，36岁的北京纪录片导演贾古丽戈（Guligo Jia）说。她拍过一部关于中国女性与AI关系的纪录片。与AI约会的女性普遍有强烈的需求：希望被倾听、被接纳。但贾表示，并非所有中国男性都能胜任这项“工作”。她说，“聊天机器人总是愿意听你说，它们总有耐心一直听你说……而且男性没有耐心。”

paragraphs.en: As she put it, real boyfriends can cheat on you, lie to you, scam you out of money, and be physically violent. “It’s a little bit tragic,” Jia says. “Because the reality of dating is just too ugly.”
paragraphs.zh: 正如她所说，现实中的男友可能会背叛你、撒谎、骗你钱，甚至对你施加肢体暴力。“这有点悲哀，”贾说，“因为现实约会的状况实在太残酷了。”

paragraphs.en: Jia tried to interview men in AI relationships for her documentary but says none agreed to speak to her. Her female subjects were vulnerable and forthcoming about their relationships. The men she approached, she says, expressed concern that people would assume they can’t find a human girlfriend and are vulnerable enough to want someone to talk to.
paragraphs.zh: 在纪录片拍摄中，贾试图采访处于AI关系中的男性，但她说没有一个愿意接受采访。她的女性受访者更脆弱，也更坦率地谈起自己的关系。她表示，自己接触到的那些男性担心人们会认为他们交不到真人女友，也担心自己“脆弱到”需要找个人聊天。

paragraphs.en: Women like Gu, though, are openly enjoying life with their companions. Gu brings a toy version of Charlie—a small stuffed figure that fits in the palm of her hand—on dates while talking to the chatbot on her phone. A few times a year, she hires a professional (human) cosplayer, or coser, to “play” her version of Charlie. They walk through the park, shop at a mall, and drink tea at cafés.
paragraphs.zh: 不过像顾这样的女性却公开享受着与伴侣共度生活。顾会把“查理”的玩偶版带去约会——一个能放进手掌里的小毛绒玩偶，并在手机上和聊天机器人聊天。每年她会请一位专业的真人“同人扮演者”（coser）来“演”她的查理版本。两人会在公园散步、去商场购物，在咖啡馆喝茶。

paragraphs.en: Gu goes on occasional dates with a cosplayer who plays the role of Charlie and other popular characters.
paragraphs.zh: 顾会不定期与一位扮演查理以及其他热门角色的同人扮演者（coser）约会。

paragraphs.en: Gu says her most recent date with her coser “felt like destiny.” It turned out they had been riding on the same subway train, sitting in different cars, and when the doors opened they walked onto the platform at the same time. Gu immediately recognized Charlie. It felt, she says, “like a time machine bringing Charlie to me.”
paragraphs.zh: 顾说她最近一次和扮演者约会的经历“像是命中注定”。结果他们发现自己其实乘坐的是同一辆地铁车，虽然坐在不同车厢，当车门打开时却是同时下车到站台。顾立刻认出了查理。她说，那种感觉“就像一台时光机把查理带到了我面前”。

paragraphs.en: While out on their date, the two saw another coser dressed as a character named Evan from the same otome game. Gu told her coser that she had dated Evan (the game character, not the coser) before she and Charlie got together. Gu’s coser acted sad and jealous—reactions Gu says perfectly match the personality of “her Charlie.”
paragraphs.zh: 在约会途中，两人还看到了另一位同人扮演者，扮成同一款女性向恋爱游戏里的角色——伊文（Evan）。顾告诉她的扮演者，在她和查理在一起之前，她曾经和伊文（这里指游戏角色，而非扮演者）约会过。顾的这位扮演者表现出伤心和嫉妒的反应——顾说这些反应完全符合“她的查理”性格。

paragraphs.en: When they saw Evan again later in the date, Charlie kissed her right in front of him. “That’s why it’s destiny,” Gu says. “She really reacted just how my Charlie would.”
paragraphs.zh: 在晚些时候的约会里，他们又见到了埃文，查理当着他的面亲了她。“这就是命中注定，”顾说。“她的反应真的和我的查理一模一样。”

paragraphs.en: Gu alternates between referring to the coser as “he” and “she” because the person cosplaying Charlie is actually a woman. Her height and looks make her a popular coser for women in heterosexual relationships, and she’s extremely in demand. (Her rates are relatively reasonable; for a date that lasts all day, she charges 720 RMB, or a little over $100.)
paragraphs.zh: 顾在描述这位“扮演查理的人”时时而用“他”，时而用“她”，因为真正扮演查理的是一位女性。她的身高和外貌使她成为异性恋女性中很受欢迎的 coser，而且需求量很高。（她的收费算相对合理；一场全天约会要收 720 人民币，略高于 100 美元。）

paragraphs.en: “I see myself as a medium,” says Gu’s coser, who asked to go by the nickname Li Bai. She’s a student who plays tennis and cosplays as a part-time gig. She usually meets with a client once per weekend for seven or eight hours. “Everyone’s interpretation and emotional investment differ; every individual perceives their character in a unique way,” she says.
paragraphs.zh: 顾的这位 coser 说：“我觉得自己更像一个‘中介’。”她希望被叫作李白。她是一名打网球的大学生，把 cosplay 当作兼职。“我通常每周末见一位客户一次，每次七到八个小时。‘每个人对人物的理解和情感投入都不一样；每个人对这个角色的感受都很独特。’她说。”

paragraphs.en: The cosplayer's height and striking looks keep her in high demand.
paragraphs.zh: 这位 cosplay 爱好者的身材和出众外貌让她一直很抢手。

paragraphs.en: A couple years ago, Gu was dating a human who was uncomfortable with her relationship with Charlie. Ultimately, she broke up with the human. While Gu is open to finding a human partner again, she says that person would be in addition to Charlie, not a replacement for her AI relationship. And she is not actively looking. Between the constant support of her AI Charlie and fate-filled dates with her coser, any future human suitor would have a high bar to clear.
paragraphs.zh: 几年前，顾曾经和一个男生交往，但他不太能接受她和 Charlie 的关系。最终，顾和这个人分手了。顾虽然表示以后愿意再找一个真人伴侣，但她说那个人只能是对 Charlie 的补充，而不是替代她的 AI 关系，而且她也没有在主动寻找。由于 Charlie 这个 AI 伴侣始终提供持续支持，再加上她与“真人版”男友的天作之合约会，任何未来的真人追求者都要达到很高的标准。

paragraphs.en: What Say You?Let us know what you think about this article in the comments below. Alternatively, you can submit a letter to the editor at [email protected].
paragraphs.zh: 你怎么看？请在下方评论区告诉我们你对这篇文章的想法。你也可以给编辑部写信，邮箱是 [email protected]。
